contents.text: The Anatomy of a Java Program
Every Java program starts with a class definition. Inside that class, you need a 'main' method. Think of the main method as the front door to your house—it is where the execution starts!
Example: public static void main(String[] args) { ... }
Variables are like named containers. In Java, you must declare what kind of data the container holds. For example, 'int' is for whole numbers, and 'double' is for decimals.
Remember: Java is strongly typed, meaning you can't put text into a number variable!
